statement: The 'safety in consumption' core (provenance manifest, SBOM, dependency/secret scan, trust labels, capability scoping, hidden-instruction lint) is already delivered, free and MIT-licensed, by Microsoft's Agent Governance Toolkit — which additionally covers runtime, signing (Ed25519), SLSA provenance and all 10 OWASP agentic risks.

evidence.quote: MIT-licensed. Addresses all 10 OWASP agentic risks. Includes SLSA-compatible build provenance, cryptographic chain from source to artifact, Dependabot scanning, pinned dependencies with cryptographic hashes, Ed25519 signing + manifest verification. MCP Security Gateway addresses tool poisoning, typosquatting, hidden instructions.
In late 2025 OWASP released a separate Top 10 for Agentic AI covering uncontrolled autonomy, delegated identity abuse, cross-agent prompt injection.

falsifier: Show the MS toolkit operates only on running agents / .NET-Microsoft-stack frameworks and does NOT validate static authored Markdown skill artefacts pre-deploy across Claude Code/Copilot/Cursor/Codex — i.e., agent-enterprise's build-time-artefact scope is a genuine gap it leaves open for heterogeneous vendor-neutral repos.
